title: The Science lesson
big_question: What did they do in the Science lesson?
skills: Reading: introduction to the characters and a Science lesson story
Listening: song about the Time Travellers
Speaking: ask and answer about a perfect day / the worst day
Writing: write a short story about a dream day
speaking_prompts: What did you do at school yesterday?
How was your day?
What time did you get up?
Imagine yesterday was your perfect day. What happened?

mini_games.prompt: You wear these over your eyes to protect them in the lab.
This is a long, thin glass container used in experiments.
goggles, apron, gloves, shelf
mini_games.answer: shelf (the others are things you wear)

mini_games.prompt: Yesterday, I _______ (get) up at seven o'clock.

title: Disaster!
phonics: split digraphs (a-e, i-e, o-e, u-e) - e.g., shake, inside, smoke, tube
skills: Reading: story about Pompeii
Listening: news report about a fire
Speaking: ask and answer about past activities
Writing: write a report about a disaster
project: Make a fact file about the South American Plate
speaking_prompts: What were you doing at 5 o'clock yesterday?
What were you doing when the earthquake happened?
What were you doing while your friend was playing?

mini_games.prompt: This is a mountain that throws out hot smoke, ash and lava.
It's a natural disaster when the ground shakes.
statue, temple, smoke, fountain

title: In the rainforest
grammar: don't have to / doesn't have to (lack of obligation)
skills: Reading: story about meeting a tribe in the rainforest
Listening: trading cards about rainforest animals
Speaking: discussion about creatures you'd like to see
Writing: description of an animal
project: Make a poster about an ecosystem
speaking_prompts: What do you have to do at home?
What did you have to do last weekend?
Which creature would you most like to see? Why?

mini_games.prompt: This big cat lives in the rainforest. It has spots.
This animal is very slow and lives in trees.
In the jungle, you _______ wear boots to protect your feet from snakes.

title: The rock 'n' roll show
big_question: What was music like in the past?
grammar: Telling the time (to the nearest five minutes)
skills: Reading: interview with a rock star
Listening: song about becoming a star
Speaking: ask and answer about future plans
Writing: diary entry about a concert
clil_topic: Music: Music genres (orchestral, jazz, hip hop)
project: Make a fact file about a famous musician
speaking_prompts: What are you going to do this weekend?
When are you going to do your homework?
What time is it? (quarter past, twenty to, etc.)

mini_games.prompt: This person sings in a concert but isn't the main singer.
You play this instrument with sticks. It has drums and cymbals.

title: Space restaurant
grammar: Zero conditional (If you put chocolate in the sun, it melts.)
skills: Reading: blog about Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
Listening: recipe for hot chocolate
Speaking: ordering food in a restaurant
Writing: write a recipe
project: Make a chart about the process of making jam
speaking_prompts: When's your birthday?
What happens if you leave ice cream in the sun?
What would you like to order?

mini_games.prompt: You use this to eat soup. It's like a small bowl on a handle.
These are small, sweet, red fruits. They're often eaten with cream.
If you _______ water, it boils at 100 degrees.

title: The Wild West
big_question: What was life like in the Wild West?
skills: Reading: story about bank robbery
Listening: song about Billy the Kid
Speaking: guessing game about objects
Writing: a Wanted poster
value: understanding and learning about other cultures
project: Make a leaflet about a material Native Americans used
speaking_prompts: What are boots made of?
What are handcuffs used for?
Whose badge is this?

mini_games.prompt: This person puts bad people in jail. They wear a star badge.
These are used to catch people. A policeman puts them on your wrists.
This cage is _______ of metal.

title: In Istanbul
big_question: What can we buy in different countries?
grammar: Questions with 'Could I...?' and 'Do you mind if I...?' (requests/permission)
skills: Reading: blog about where things are made
Listening: dialogue about choosing a present
Speaking: buying souvenirs, asking for permission
Writing: a leaflet giving advice to visitors
value: showing interest in the wider world
speaking_prompts: What should you do in a new city?
What shouldn't you do?
Could I try this on?
Do you mind if I open the window?

mini_games.prompt: You wear these on your ears. They're usually made of metal.
You use this to make your hair neat. It has teeth.
You _______ visit the Blue Mosque. It's beautiful!

title: The story teller
big_question: What do we know about the theatre?
grammar: Present perfect with just (I've just heard...)
phonics: Silent 'e' at the end of words (make, scene, promise, theatre)
skills: Reading: story about helping Shakespeare
Listening: interview about the Globe Theatre
Speaking: making offers and promises
Writing: a short biography
project: Make a theatre stage in a box
speaking_prompts: I'm thirsty. (I'll get you a drink.)
This bag is heavy. (I'll help you.)
What has just happened?

mini_games.prompt: This is a person who performs in a play or film.
These are the people who watch a play.
Don't worry, I _______ help you with your homework.

title: Museum of the future
big_question: How will jobs change in the future?
skills: Reading: future jobs
Listening: robot advert
Speaking: robot game (If you're hungry, I'll make you a sandwich)
Writing: a poem using first conditional
speaking_prompts: If you're tired, what will you do?
If it rains tomorrow, what will you do?
If you're hungry, the robot will...

mini_games.prompt: This person designs buildings.
This person looks after your teeth.
If you _______ thirsty, I'll get you a glass of water.

title: Mystery at sea
big_question: What do we know about the sea?
grammar: Present perfect with already and yet
Present perfect questions with yet (Have you ... yet?)
Present perfect with just (for recent events)
skills: Reading: story about the mystery of the Mary Celeste
Reading: article about the Bermuda Triangle
Listening: song about Grandma falling in the sea
Listening: radio show about mythical sea creatures
Speaking: ask and answer about things you have (not) done
Writing: a description of an imaginary sea monster
Writing: a summary of the Time Travellers' story
project: Make a fact file about a marine animal
speaking_prompts: Have you done your homework yet?
Have you ever seen a UFO?
What do you think about the Bermuda Triangle?
What has happened on the ship?

mini_games.prompt: This person is in charge of a ship.
This is a round window on a ship.
You use this to check the weather on a ship.
I've _______ (see) that film. It was great!
Have you finished your breakfast _______?
captain, sailor, barometer, pirate
